arguments.title: Precedent Support - Prosecution
arguments.content: Argument supporting prosecution position citing similar cases ['2024_AppealCourt_September_criminal_judgment_1151', '2023_AppealCourt_March_criminal_judgment_1166', '2021_AppealCourt_March_criminal_judgment_1155']. Include specific judge statements from model patterns if available.
judge_statements: The court has consistently held that fraudulent activities will not be tolerated
The accused's actions demonstrate a clear intent to deceive and misappropriate funds
legal_principles: The principle of fraud and misappropriation of funds is strictly prohibited under the law
The court will not hesitate to impose severe penalties for such offenses
model_extracted_points: The accused's admission of guilt initially and subsequent retraction of the statement can be seen as an attempt to deceive the court
The presence of forged documents and CCTV footage corroborates the prosecution's case

arguments.title: Precedent Support - Defense
arguments.content: Argument supporting defense position citing similar cases. Include specific judge statements from model patterns if available.
judge_names: V. Akmeemana Obata Mudalige Ananda Wijesena
judge_statements: The court must consider the possibility of false accusations and ensure the accused's rights are protected
The prosecution must provide conclusive evidence to prove the accused's guilt beyond a reasonable doubt
legal_principles: The principle of innocent until proven guilty must be upheld
The court must carefully evaluate the evidence presented to ensure a fair trial
model_extracted_points: The accused's change in statement can be attributed to coercion or misunderstanding
The lack of direct evidence linking the accused to the crime raises doubts about their guilt

arguments.title: Precedent Support - Mitigating
arguments.content: Argument for mitigating factors citing similar cases. Include specific judge statements from model patterns if available.
judge_statements: The court may consider mitigating factors such as the accused's cooperation and remorse
The severity of the sentence must be proportionate to the offense committed
legal_principles: The principle of proportionality in sentencing must be upheld
The court must consider the accused's character and potential for rehabilitation
model_extracted_points: The accused's initial admission of guilt and subsequent cooperation with the investigation can be seen as a mitigating factor
The absence of prior convictions and the accused's good character can support a more lenient sentence

arguments.title: Precedent Support - Aggressive
arguments.content: Aggressive argument citing binding precedents. Include specific judge statements from model patterns if available.
judge_names: V. Akmeemana Obata Mudalige Ananda Wijesena
judge_statements: The court has consistently held that severe penalties will be imposed for fraudulent activities
The accused's actions demonstrate a clear intent to deceive and misappropriate funds, warranting a harsh sentence
legal_principles: The principle of fraud and misappropriation of funds is strictly prohibited under the law and will be severely punished
The court will not hesitate to impose maximum penalties for such offenses
model_extracted_points: The accused's actions constitute a serious breach of trust and warrant a severe sentence
The presence of forged documents and CCTV footage provides conclusive evidence of the accused's guilt, supporting an aggressive prosecution
